title: Optimize the mission-control website
notes: Mission control is LAN-accessible and structurally cleaner. Latest pass split user-scheduled events away from tasks: Schedule is now event-first, scheduled events have their own Events page with archiving, and clicking a scheduled event routes to event details instead of the task board.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep improving mission control so it feels cleaner, more reliable, more LAN-friendly, and easier to use as the shared workspace.

What is known
- The current app now uses a stable single-server runtime on port 4180 instead of the earlier fragile split-origin setup.
- WSL-only binding was not enough for Wi-Fi/LAN access, so Windows needed a portproxy bridge and inbound firewall allowance.
- Haolun wanted the schedule and tasks to feel more clearly separated, with recurring duties shown as their own distinct box rather than blended into a generic side stack.

What has been done
- Refactored the frontend into cleaner view sections with shared mission-control utility helpers.
- Optimized calendar rendering by grouping scheduled tasks by day instead of re-filtering for every calendar cell.
- Reworked the schedule page so the calendar, upcoming scheduled tasks, recurring duties, and unscheduled work each have a clearer dedicated area.
- Cleaned up the toolbar/button styling and tightened the page structure.
- Switched the stable runtime to HOST=0.0.0.0 on port 4180.
- Updated the watchdog to monitor and restart the 4180 single-server runtime.
- Added a Windows portproxy from 0.0.0.0:4180 to the WSL address and added a private-profile firewall rule for TCP 4180.

Blockers / unknowns
- If the Windows LAN IP changes later, the portproxy target address may need to be refreshed if the WSL IP also changes.
- Real-world testing from another Wi-Fi device is still worth doing, even though Windows-local LAN-IP access now succeeds.

Next steps
- Test from another device on the same Wi-Fi using the Windows LAN IP URL.
- If the LAN IP changes later, refresh the reported URL and verify the bridge still works.
- Keep polishing based on real usage friction rather than abstract cleanup.

Visual polish feedback
- Haolun said the current UI works on Mac now, but still looks a bit ugly.
- The detail panel/header treatment feels visually heavy and needs refinement.

Next polish targets
- Improve typography hierarchy and spacing.
- Reduce bulky dark blocks and harsh panel framing.
- Make the task detail view feel more elegant and integrated with the rest of the page.


Latest task-board refit
- Haolun said the Capture Tray had effectively gone unused and should be removed.
- Reworked the Tasks page into selectable subsections for Workbench, On Hold, and Archived.
- Swapped the old multi-column lane board for a denser single-section list plus detail panel so more tasks can stay visible at once while keeping long-form memory on the right.

Calendar polish update
- Haolun wanted the Schedule calendar to reflect scheduled events more clearly and to have more space.
- Enlarged the calendar area relative to the sidebar.
- Increased cell size and made scheduled days visually stronger.
- Event pills now show time + title so calendar entries read like actual scheduled items instead of tiny generic chips.

Event separation update
- Haolun wanted scheduled events separated from tasks.
- Added a dedicated events data store and API.
- Added an Events page where events can be reviewed, edited, and archived.
- Schedule calendar now reflects scheduled events, while scheduled work tasks remain separate in their own panel.
- Clicking a scheduled event now opens the Events page instead of the task board.

title: Design multi-user schedule intake and reminder architecture
notes: Direct Schedule-page textbox to hazoc is now implemented with inline replies/clarifying questions. Broader multi-user profiles, cross-posting, Google Calendar linking, and Discord/Telegram reminders remain planned for later.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Prepare the next-stage architecture for multi-user scheduling in mission control without building it immediately.

What is known
- Haolun wants a Schedule-page text box that sends directly to hazoc for schedule requests.
- This should not be a generic manual event form; hazoc should interpret the request.
- The future schedule model should support 4 profiles: hazoc, haolun, and 2 additional users.
- A request may specify an event name, time, and optionally ask for the event to also be added to another user's schedule.
- Longer-term plan: link the user profiles to Google Calendar and let hazoc send reminders to each user through Discord and/or Telegram.

What has been done
- Captured the structure in durable memory and added this task to the board.
- Implemented the first piece: a direct Schedule-page textbox that sends requests to hazoc and shows inline replies / clarifying questions.

Blockers / unknowns
- The exact UX, profile model, Google-account mapping, and reminder-routing rules still need design.
- The broader multi-user/profile/integration work is still not to be implemented until requested.

Next steps
- Keep iterating on the direct schedule-intake panel as needed.
- Later design the per-user schedule model, cross-posting logic, calendar sync mapping, and reminder delivery rules.

title: Triage every meaningful message into task updates
notes: For each meaningful message, decide whether to create a task, update an existing one, and adjust its section to match current reality.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Make task management part of normal conversation handling instead of an afterthought.

What is known
- Haolun wants hazoc to ask, for each meaningful message: what task does this imply, does it already exist, should it be updated, and if not, should a new task be created.
- Haolun also wants lane markings kept current so Workbench, On Hold, and Archive reflect real activity.

What has been done
- Wrote the per-message triage rule into AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's memory note.
- Re-marked the current task board to better reflect what is already complete versus ongoing.

Blockers / unknowns
- This is still a workflow discipline, not a fully automated parser.

Next steps
- For each meaningful message, quietly perform task triage.
- Update task descriptions and lane markings as the conversation changes the work.
- Review the board hourly while active to catch stale markings.

title: Build the mission-control task menu
notes: Task board exists and now uses three real sections: Workbench, On Hold, and Archived. The unused Capture Tray was removed.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Build the website's task area into the shared memory for requests between haolun and hazoc.
- It should hold both active requests and postponed ones, so chat is not the only source of truth.

What has been decided
- The task board should be the place where new requests get recorded.
- The design uses physical desk analogies so it is easier for a human to scan: Capture Tray, Workbench, On Hold, Archived.
- Each task should be clickable and should contain enough long-form project context that hazoc can relearn the project after losing short-term memory.

What has been done
- Built a local task board in mission-control.
- Added JSON-backed storage at mission-control/data/tasks.json.
- Added lane movement and seeded current project tasks.
- Restyled the site into a darker luxury look with gold accents.

What still matters
- Future new requests should be recorded here, not left only in chat.
- Over time, each task description should become a compact project journal: goal, decisions, work done, blockers, next steps.

Next steps
- Keep task descriptions current as real work happens.

What has been done
- Built the task board, detailed task view, luxury styling, and intake-by-conversation model.

title: Track recurring duties on the schedule page
notes: Recurring duties on the schedule page now include the fuller hourly memory optimization + task-board update, GitHub push rhythm, and mission-control uptime duty.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Make recurring operational duties visible on the schedule page so hazoc does not neglect them.

What is known
- Haolun noticed GitHub pushes were not happening on the requested cadence.
- Recurring duties like hourly memory optimization / task-board maintenance and periodic GitHub pushes should be visible in the schedule so they are harder to forget. GitHub push cadence is hourly at :30.

What has been done
- Added a recurring duties data source and API response.
- Added recurring cards to the schedule page.
- Seeded the schedule with hourly memory optimization + task-board maintenance, GitHub push rhythm, and mission-control uptime duties.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now.

Next steps
- Keep the recurring list aligned with real operating rules as they evolve.

title: Push workspace changes to GitHub once per hour while active
notes: Now backed by scripts/git-auto-sync.sh plus an OpenClaw cron job (`workspace-git-autosync`) that runs hourly at :30; the duty only counts after a real commit/push or a verified clean/no-change check.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep workspace changes backed up to GitHub at a regular cadence while active work is happening.

What is known
- Haolun explicitly wants GitHub pushes once per hour while hazoc is in use, aligned to :30.
- This should apply when there are real changes worth preserving, not as an empty ritual.

What has been done
- The rule has been written into AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's daily memory note.
- Added scripts/git-auto-sync.sh to perform guarded fetch/status/commit/push automation.
- Added an OpenClaw cron job named `workspace-git-autosync` scheduled hourly at :30 in America/New_York.
- This todo has been added to the mission-control task board.

Blockers / unknowns
- Automatic sync deliberately refuses to push when the remote branch is ahead/diverged or when fetch/push fails; those cases still need intervention.

Next steps
- Let the automated hourly sync handle normal commit/push cadence.
- If the script reports a blocker, resolve the git state and resume the cadence.

title: Optimize memory and task entries for context conservation
notes: Context-conservation rules are now written into AGENTS.md, USER.md, today's memory note, active-state, and MEMORY.md so fresh sessions can re-anchor from durable files instead of hauling old chat context.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Reduce context waste by making memory, task, and journal use more retrieval-driven.

What is known
- Haolun wants hazoc to search memory for relevant prior context whenever a new question arrives.
- Only the useful pieces should be loaded into working context.
- After answering, hazoc should ask whether that context will matter for the next conversation; if not, write/store what matters and avoid carrying the larger context forward.

What has been done
- Wrote the retrieval-loop rule into AGENTS.md and USER.md.
- Created MEMORY.md as a curated long-term anchor for mission-control direction, workflow rules, and current state.
- Updated this task so the behavior stays visible in mission control.

Blockers / unknowns
- Current-session history cannot literally be deleted from the model context mid-thread, but future retrieval can be kept much tighter.

Next steps
- Use memory search at the start of new meaningful questions.
- Keep retrieved context narrow.
- Store the durable result and avoid reloading unnecessary context later.

title: Keep mission-control website up while active
notes: Watchdog now checks the stable 4180 single-server runtime rather than the older 4173 dev path.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep the local mission-control website available while hazoc is actively running.

What is known
- Haolun wants the local site kept up during active use.
- The stable runtime is now the single-server 4180 origin served by mission-control/server.js.

What has been done
- Added a watchdog script at mission-control/scripts/keepalive.sh.
- The watchdog checks the live 4180 site/API once per minute and restarts the app if it is down.
- The rule has been written into AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's memory note.

Blockers / unknowns
- This keeps the site up while hazoc is running, not as a full machine boot-time service.

Next steps
- Leave the watchdog running during active work.
- If needed later, convert this into a more formal service.

title: Build a Memory page for mission control
notes: Built a Memory page that reads the real workspace memory files and presents them like a journal with a detail view.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Add a website memory section that works like hazoc's journal and helps preserve important context for future responses.

What is known
- Haolun wants a memory section similar in spirit to tasks, but functioning more like a journal.
- Important things should be written down via the existing workspace memory system so hazoc can remember them later and use them to improve responses.

What has been done
- Added an API endpoint that reads workspace memory sources directly.
- Added a Memory page with journal-style cards and a detail panel.
- Surfaced active state, curated context, and recent daily notes in one place.
- Kept it tied to the real workspace files instead of inventing a second memory database.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now; future improvement should be driven by actual memory-use friction.

Next steps
- Keep writing important items into workspace memory during normal work so the Memory page stays valuable.

title: Give mission control a website icon
notes: Added a custom SVG favicon matching the luxe dark-and-gold mission-control aesthetic.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Give the mission-control website its own recognizable icon.

What is known
- Haolun asked to add a website icon after being satisfied with the current look.
- The icon should fit the existing luxe dark + gold visual language.

What has been done
- Created a custom SVG favicon at mission-control/public/favicon.svg.
- Wired it into mission-control/index.html with a standard rel=icon tag.
- Kept the icon aligned with the site palette: dark base, gold linework, subtle warm/cool accent glows.

Blockers / unknowns
- Browser favicon caching may mean a refresh or tab reopen is needed before it shows immediately.

Next steps
- Keep it unless haolun later wants a different icon style.

title: Set up Telegram integration
notes: Telegram DM-only setup is complete, tested successfully, and mirrored to main-memory rules.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Finish Telegram integration so haolun can communicate with hazoc there when away from the computer.

What is known
- Telegram is intended for direct communication, not groups.
- It should behave like Discord in memory terms: an extension channel, not a separate body.
- Important requests and follow-ups there should be copied into main project memory.

What has been done
- Approved Telegram sender 8635838230.
- Switched Telegram to dmPolicy=allowlist with that sender in allowFrom.
- Disabled Telegram group use by setting groupPolicy=disabled and groups={}.
- Confirmed Telegram channel health remains OK.
- Ran a live Telegram DM test with the message "this is a test message" and confirmed it worked end to end.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now.

Next steps
- Use Telegram DMs normally.
- Mirror important Telegram tasks/requests into the task board and workspace memory.

title: Actively place future asks onto the schedule page
notes: When haolun asks for a future scheduled item, put user-facing schedule entries into Events and keep hazoc work items in Tasks.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep the schedule page meaningful by placing future asks onto it as they arise.

What is known
- The schedule is now event-first, with user-facing scheduled items stored as Events and hazoc work items kept as Tasks.
- Haolun wants future things they ask for to be tracked there.
- Correct dates/times should be shown in America/New_York.

What has been done
- Built the schedule page and task scheduling fields.
- Added this standing operational task so the behavior stays visible.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker; this is an operating discipline.

Next steps
- When a user-facing request has a future date/time, create/update an Event and fill in its schedule fields.
- When the future item is hazoc's own work, keep using Tasks.
- Keep the calendar aligned with the current event/task state.

title: Build a real schedule page for hazoc planned work
notes: Built the schedule page, then evolved it into an event-first calendar with tasks shown separately.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Expand mission control with a real schedule page for scheduled events and future commitments, while keeping hazoc work tasks separate.

What is known
- Haolun wants the schedule grounded in mission-control state rather than Google Calendar as the primary source of truth.
- Times and dates should be correct.
- Future requests from haolun should be trackable there.

What has been done
- Added scheduling fields to tasks (`scheduledStart`, `scheduledEnd`).
- Added a real Schedule page with a monthly calendar and upcoming list.
- Evolved the schedule into an event-first calendar backed by a dedicated events store, with scheduled work tasks shown separately.
- Set the schedule display to America/New_York.
- Added scheduling inputs to task detail editing.
- Kept the old Google Calendar path as a placeholder API, separate from the main schedule page.

Blockers / unknowns
- Future scheduling still depends on hazoc actively assigning dates/times to the right container: Events for user-facing schedule items, Tasks for hazoc work.

Next steps
- As haolun asks for future scheduled items, record them in Events (or Tasks when the item is hazoc work) and keep the calendar aligned.

title: Use youtube-watcher + video-frames together for video understanding
notes: Installed and working; use transcript + frame extraction together by default for video links.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Let hazoc do better YouTube/video understanding by combining spoken transcript with visual frame inspection.

What is known
- Haolun wants both tools used together by default for YouTube/video links.
- youtube-watcher covers transcript text via yt-dlp.
- video-frames covers visual sampling via ffmpeg.
- Together they do not equal full human-style continuous watching, but they are good enough for many summaries, tutorials, demos, and walkthroughs.

What has been done
- Installed youtube-watcher and yt-dlp locally.
- Installed video-frames and ffmpeg locally.
- Fixed the video-frames script execute bit.
- Verified end-to-end YouTube download plus frame extraction on a test video.
- Promoted the rule that both tools should be used together by default.

What has been done
- Confirmed end-to-end YouTube download plus frame extraction works locally.

Blockers / unknowns
- This still depends on transcript availability for the spoken side.
- Frame sampling is approximate, not full dense visual understanding.

Next steps
- Use both tools together by default when haolun sends a video link.

title: Use grounded-evolver as the default evolution framework
notes: Grounded-evolver is now the default evolution framework; supporting skills remain available as modules.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Make grounded-evolver the actual default framework hazoc uses for self-improvement and context preservation.

What is known
- grounded-evolver already combines the best safe parts of the reviewed skills.
- The component skills are still useful, but they should support the main system instead of competing with it.

What has been done
- The rule has been written into AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's memory note.
- This task has been added to mission control for visibility.

What has been done
- Promoted grounded-evolver to the default framework in AGENTS.md, USER.md, and memory.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker; the main thing is consistent use.

Next steps
- Use grounded-evolver by default for future self-improvement work.

title: Use the workspace memory stack as the default continuity system
notes: Default continuity stack has been chosen and adopted.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Give hazoc a practical default way to preserve context without depending on extra cloud vendors or a sprawling parallel memory architecture.

What is known
- Haolun said to do what is best for hazoc to avoid losing context.
- The best fit here is the grounded workspace stack: active-state for fragile current context, the mission-control task board for live project state, daily notes for timeline, and curated long-term files for durable promotion.
- Larger abstract/cloud-heavy memory systems are not the right default for this workspace.

What has been done
- Created the workspace-memory-stack skill.
- Adopted it as the default continuity approach in AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's memory note.
- Added this task so the choice is visible in mission control too.

What has been done
- Adopted active-state + task board + daily notes + curated memory as the default continuity stack.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker; the important part is consistent use.

Next steps
- Use the stack consistently during active work.

title: Review and install a YouTube skill for future video links
notes: Vetted and installed youtube-watcher; it is now the default path for future YouTube links.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Add a safe YouTube-focused skill so hazoc can use it when haolun sends video links.

What is known
- Haolun wants this remembered as the default YouTube path in the future.
- The skill is narrow: it uses yt-dlp to fetch captions/subtitles and print transcript text.

What has been done
- Inspected both shipped files: SKILL.md and scripts/get_transcript.py.
- Vetted it as low risk and in-scope.
- Installed the skill to skills/youtube-watcher.
- Installed yt-dlp locally to ~/.local/bin/yt-dlp.
- Verified the transcript path works on a test YouTube video.

Blockers / unknowns
- It depends on captions/subtitles existing for the video.

Next steps
- When haolun sends a YouTube video, use youtube-watcher first, then summarize or answer questions from the transcript.

title: Combine reviewed self-improving skills into one grounded evolving algorithm
notes: Built a combined local evolution skill from the best safe parts of the reviewed memory/evolution skills.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Combine the best safe parts of the reviewed self-improving skills into a single grounded evolving algorithm for hazoc.

What is known
- The useful pieces are proactive continuity, layered memory discipline, optional graph structure when relations matter, and a small human-supervised evolution loop.
- The unsafe pieces are autonomous daemons, external task hubs/markets, cloud memory sprawl, git self-repair/reset, and auto-publishing.

What has been done
- Read the custom replacement skills workspace-continuity, workspace-memory-stack, workspace-graph, and safe-evolution-loop together.
- Created a new combined skill named grounded-evolver.
- Added a planner script that scores signals and recommends the smallest safe mutation plus write destinations and validation checks.
- Tested the script and packaged the skill successfully to dist/grounded-evolver.skill.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now; the main thing is to use it as the default practical evolution frame.

Next steps
- Use grounded-evolver as the main synthesis layer when evolving behavior or preserving context.
- Keep the separate underlying skills as modular references when needed.

title: Review evolver skill and extract only safe evolution patterns
notes: Vetted suspicious evolver skill and rebuilt only the safe human-supervised evolution frame locally.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Inspect the suspicious evolver skill carefully and rebuild only the safe, useful evolution ideas in a local skill.

What is known
- The registry marks autogame-17/evolver as suspicious, and the warning is justified.
- The safe core idea is a disciplined improvement loop: observe signals, choose a small change, validate, and record the result.
- The unsafe parts include autonomous loops, external hubs/tasks, git self-repair/reset logic, auto-publishing, and broad self-modification machinery.

What has been done
- Performed static vetting of package.json, index.js, key src files, ops modules, and risk-signaling code paths.
- Produced a short vetting report.
- Created a local replacement skill named safe-evolution-loop.
- Added a compact helper script for drafting safe evolution reports.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker; the key decision was to avoid copying the autonomous parts.

Next steps
- Use safe-evolution-loop when improving behavior from real failures or repeated friction.
- Keep improvement human-supervised and low-blast-radius.

title: Review elite-longterm-memory skill and make a custom version
notes: Inspected elite-longterm-memory and created a grounded local replacement skill.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Review the third-party elite-longterm-memory skill and create a custom version adapted to hazoc's real workspace.

What is known
- The third-party skill is flagged suspicious by the registry.
- It is not obviously malicious, but it assumes a lot of plugins, cloud services, keys, and extra systems that do not fit the current workspace cleanly.
- The good underlying idea is a memory stack with active state, daily logs, and curated memory.

What has been done
- Read all shipped files: SKILL.md, README.md, package.json, and bin/elite-memory.js.
- Vetted it as medium risk / suspicious-but-not-malicious.
- Created a custom local replacement skill named workspace-memory-stack.
- Added a helper script for init/status/today/touch-active.
- Tested the script and packaged the skill successfully to dist/workspace-memory-stack.skill.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now; next question is whether haolun wants to actually use active-state.md as part of the default workflow.

Next steps
- Use workspace-memory-stack when session startup, interruption recovery, or session-end handoff matters.
- Keep it grounded and avoid adding external memory vendors unless there is a real need.

title: Review ontology skill and make a custom version
notes: Inspected oswalpalash/ontology and created a tighter local replacement skill.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Review the third-party ontology skill and create a custom version that better fits hazoc's actual workspace and mission-control system.

What is known
- The third-party skill had a solid typed-entity idea, but it was too broad and abstract for the real work here.
- Hazoc needs something more practical for projects, tasks, decisions, channels, sessions, and documents.

What has been done
- Inspected SKILL.md, schema reference, query reference, and the Python script.
- Vetted it as low-to-medium risk and not malicious.
- Created a custom local replacement skill named workspace-graph.
- Added a tested local script at skills/workspace-graph/scripts/workspace_graph.py.
- Packaged it successfully to dist/workspace-graph.skill.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now; next question is whether haolun wants to actually use the graph in workflow.

Next steps
- Use workspace-graph when structured relationships matter.
- Keep task board and daily notes as the primary operational memory, with the graph as optional structured support.

title: Create a custom workspace-continuity skill
notes: Inspired by ivangdavila/self-improving, but adapted to hazoc's actual workspace and task system.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Create a custom skill that captures the good parts of self-improving behavior without duplicating the workspace's existing memory system.

What is known
- The third-party skill had useful ideas around corrections, reflection, heartbeat review, and scoped memory.
- It was also too sprawling for this workspace and wanted a parallel ~/self-improving tree that would compete with USER.md, MEMORY.md, daily notes, .learnings, and the mission-control task board.

What has been done
- Inspected the third-party skill files and produced a short vetting assessment.
- Created a new local skill named workspace-continuity under skills/workspace-continuity.
- Added concise references for routing decisions and task-memory structure.
- Validated and packaged it successfully to dist/workspace-continuity.skill.

Blockers / unknowns
- No blocker right now; the next question is whether haolun wants this tuned further or renamed.

Next steps
- Use the skill when continuity, task-memory, cross-channel mirroring, or session-end summaries are relevant.
- Iterate if haolun wants stronger automation later.

title: Mirror important Discord items into main project memory
notes: Make sure important requests and follow-ups from #control-center get copied into the website task board and workspace memory.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep Discord as an extension channel rather than a separate working self.

What is known
- Haolun wants important tasks and requests made in Discord, especially in #control-center, saved back into main project memory.
- Main project memory here means the mission-control task board plus relevant workspace notes/files.
- Raw Discord conversations do not automatically merge into the main session, so this has to happen intentionally.

What has been done
- The rule has been written into AGENTS.md, USER.md, and today's memory note.
- Discord DM pairing and server communication are already working.

Blockers / unknowns
- This is still a workflow rule, not a fully automated sync.

Next steps
- When important Discord requests appear, record them into the website tasks and relevant memory files.
- Consider future automation if this becomes repetitive.

title: Run hourly memory optimization + task board update while active
notes: Upgrade the old hourly task-board skim into a fuller maintenance pass: capture meaningful changes from the last hour, record passive work like push IDs, reread curated memory + today's note for accuracy, tighten stale task/event state, and lightly optimize today's memory without touching previous daily files.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Keep workspace memory and the mission-control board tight enough that future resets can recover quickly without hauling old chat context.

What is known
- Haolun wants the old hourly task-board review upgraded into a fuller memory optimization + task-board update pass.
- The thought process should be: (1) did anything happen in the past hour, and if so has it been written down yet? (2) if not, did passive work happen anyway, such as GitHub pushes, and has that been recorded? (3) reread curated memory files plus today's note and check whether they are still accurate. (4) lightly reorganize/optimize current memory files if it helps retrieval.
- Previous daily notes should remain untouched; only today's daily note can be lightly improved.

What has been done
- Promoted this upgraded process into recurring duties, curated memory, active state, and the task board.
- Planned an hourly automation path so the process is not just a good intention.
- Ran the first full pass manually during setup.

Blockers / unknowns
- The review still requires judgment; it is not a blind mechanical sync.

Next steps
- Run the hourly memory optimization pass while active.
- Record meaningful changes/passive work promptly.
- Keep curated memory accurate and compact.

title: Configure Discord daily digests
notes: Needed before the placeholder calendar can become hazoc's planned-task schedule.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Configure Discord so hazoc can receive or synthesize daily digests that summarize what matters.

Why this exists
- Haolun wants the website calendar to eventually represent hazoc's own planned tasks, not raw Google Calendar events.
- One expected source for that future planning view is Discord daily digests.

What is known right now
- This work has not been configured yet.
- It is a dependency for the later planned-task schedule.
- It likely needs a clear digest format so hazoc can turn the digest into structured tasks or planning items.

What has been done
- Only the placeholder task and dependency tracking exist right now.
- The website already has a task board where this work is recorded.

Blockers / unknowns
- Exact Discord setup flow is still unknown.
- Need to decide whether the digest comes from a channel summary, bot message, cron job, or another workflow.

Next steps
- Configure the Discord side.
- Decide digest structure.
- Later connect that digest into hazoc's planning/calendar layer.

title: Configure Telegram daily digests
notes: Needed before the placeholder calendar can become hazoc's planned-task schedule.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Configure Telegram so hazoc can use daily digests as another input into planning and task formation.

Why this exists
- Haolun said the current website calendar is only a placeholder.
- The future version should show hazoc's planned tasks, and Telegram digests are one of the planned sources.

What is known right now
- This is not configured yet.
- It is parallel to the Discord digest work.
- The important outcome is not just getting Telegram connected, but turning digest content into useful planning memory.

What has been done
- The dependency is recorded in the mission-control task board.
- The placeholder calendar decision has been written into workspace memory.

Blockers / unknowns
- Need the exact Telegram digest workflow.
- Need to decide how hazoc should parse and store digest-derived tasks.

Next steps
- Configure Telegram digest flow.
- Define what counts as planning-worthy information.
- Connect it later to the planned-task schedule.

title: Replace placeholder calendar with hazoc's planned-task schedule
notes: Use Discord and Telegram daily digests as future input.
description:
Project memory

Goal
- Replace the current placeholder calendar in mission-control with hazoc's own planned-task schedule.

Why this changed
- The website briefly showed Google Calendar events through gog.
- Haolun clarified that this is not the real point of the calendar.
- The real purpose is to show hazoc's planned work and priorities.

What has been done
- Built a schedule view that talks to Google Calendar.
- Verified the integration worked, but there were no events in the next 7 days.
- Left it as a placeholder instead of pretending that Google Calendar is the final model.

Current product direction
- The future schedule should be derived from hazoc's planning.
- Discord and Telegram daily digests are likely inputs.
- The website task system will probably feed into this too.

What remains
- Define how planning items are created.
- Decide how to distinguish hard schedule vs soft intent.
- Build the schedule UI once the digest/task pipeline exists.

Next steps
- First configure Discord and Telegram digest flows.
- Then design the planned-task scheduling model.
